nodes.title: The Sphere
nodes.summary: The Sphere is the structure that holds every world. The countless realms of existence are stacked together as Stratums rather than scattered through empty space, layers of reality bound into a single vast whole. Each Stratum is a world complete unto itself, with its own skies and its own buried history, and most who live within one never learn that any other exists. To them, their world is all there is.

nodes.title: Akkoroka
nodes.summary: Akkoroka is a world built like a proof. Geometric towers rise in cold exactitude over alchemical vaults, harmonic geometric resonance hums through the air at frequencies that set the teeth on edge, and everything is arranged according to a sacred order of angle and ratio. The Flayed pursue perfection here through chemistry and shape, and the pursuit has a way of leaving its subjects altered, masked, and not entirely sure what they used to be.

nodes.title: Amaranthia
nodes.summary: Amaranthia is a world that never fully sets. Its cities of luminous marble hold the glow of a perpetual dawn, and the air carries the warm hum of choir and bell. To stand in its great plazas is to feel watched over, bathed in a light that feels less like sun and more like memory made visible. The Rysen call it a blessing. They are also very careful never to let it go out.

nodes.title: Avemriol
nodes.summary: Avemriol is a world of the open sky. Floating spires and sky monasteries hang among the clouds, wind flutes and aerial chorales ride the gusts, and the Strixle live their whole lives aloft, with nothing beneath them but more sky and the long fall. Storms are the great power and the great terror here, and everything the Strixle build is shaped by the wind that could just as easily carry it away.

nodes.title: Brimsever
nodes.summary: Brimsever is a world of ash fortresses and volcanic trenches, where the ground runs hot and the only law anyone fully respects is strength. War drums and furnace chants echo off the trench walls, and the people carry their history on their skin in burns and brands. Survival here is a contest that never ends, and weakness is treated not as misfortune but as the beginning of extinction.

nodes.title: Burhallow
nodes.summary: Burhallow is a cozy world with a long shadow under it. The Harfolk live across rolling green hills and sprawling underground warren-cities, bound together less by walls or borders than by stories, shared meals, and the steady belief that every life helps shape what the world becomes. Through all of it runs the Echochurn, a resonance that carries luck and possibility from one end of Burhallow to the other. Most Harfolk treat it as a natural wonder, a blessing that nudges the world toward brighter days.

nodes.title: Crixalis
nodes.summary: Crixalis is a world of hives. Hive spires and glass nests rise over cocoon tunnels that wind through the dark, clicking percussion and hive resonance pulse through every chamber, and the Trinumite live their lives as part of broods far larger than any individual. Identity here is a shared thing, held in the collective memory of the hive, and the worst fate a Trinumite can imagine is to be cut off from it.

nodes.title: Eldarwyn
nodes.summary: Eldarwyn is an ancient forest world, older and stiller than any place has a right to be. Vast primeval woods stretch unbroken between root temples, and ancient chants and spirit songs drift between the trunks like something the forest itself is remembering. The Sylvakith live among these woods as guests as much as inhabitants, and time moves strangely under the old canopy, slow and deep, so that centuries can pass like a long afternoon.

nodes.title: Elysara
nodes.summary: Elysara is a world of blooming sanctums and radiant gardens, holy ground where the air is sweet with pollen and the light falls like a blessing. Hymns and pollen flutes drift between the sanctuaries, and the Hallowed move through it as pilgrims, devoted to the sacred bloom that gives their world its grace. It is beautiful and reverent, a place built around compassion and sacrifice, and beneath the radiance runs a quiet fear that even holiness can rot.

nodes.title: Felidae
nodes.summary: Felidae is a world that never quite holds still. Light bends through it in ribbons of color, splitting off prismatic walls and pooling in the streets, so that a single lantern can throw a dozen shadows and none of them point the same way. It is a realm of palaces and gambling dens, of laughter carried on the night air and luck treated as something you can hear coming. To the people who live here, fortune is not an abstraction. It is weather. It rolls in, it turns, and the wise learn to read the sky.

nodes.title: Garyx
nodes.summary: Garyx is a world split between fire and ice and at war with itself over which should win. Frigid fortresses stand cracked across volcanic ice ruins, where glaciers grind against magma and steam never stops rising. The Nyseen live along that seam, in clans whose rivalries echo the war of the elements themselves, and they have learned that survival means holding two opposite forces in balance rather than choosing one.

nodes.title: Karn
nodes.summary: Karn is a realm of crimson skies and thunder-scarred earth, where every horizon is lined with the broken ribs of mountains and the husks of beasts once thought eternal. Valleys lie gouged by the charge of titans. Forests twist upward like bone spires. The wind carries the iron tang of blood, and during the Threnody Season it howls with the voices of battles long past, as though the land refuses to forget its dead.

nodes.title: Kwuhara
nodes.summary: Kwuhara is a world of inverted gemstone cities, citadels that hang downward from vast crystal ceilings and catch the light in cold, regal facets. Resonant crystal choirs ring through the hanging streets, and everything, from a citizen's station to their next life, is measured in the gems. It is beautiful and merciless in equal measure, a realm where order is total and the cost of that order is rarely spoken aloud.

nodes.title: Mireleor
nodes.summary: Mireleor is a thunder-split world of molten veins, rust-choked ruins, and overgrown forgelands where ancient machines sleep beneath tangled roots. The skies hang heavy with smoke and static, casting shifting light over jagged ridgelines and half-buried gears the size of fortresses. Jungle canopies crackle with electric wind, and the plains tremble with the tread of something colossal just out of sight.

nodes.title: Mireth
nodes.summary: Mireth is a vast jungle world where civilization grows as much as it is built. The Fribbit raise their city-states among colossal living trees and stepped pyramids beneath a canopy so deep the sun arrives as a green dusk, and through every root, ruin, and rooftop bloom the Xochitli, sacred flowers infused with the lingering essence of ancient jungle gods. The blooms are the heart of everything here. They feed the people, heal the wounded, arm the warriors, and carry the faith, and the art of unlocking them is the foundation the whole world stands on.

nodes.title: Mortavia
nodes.summary: Mortavia is a world that treats death as a doorway it keeps oiled and ready. Funeral monasteries and bone cathedrals stand over candle crypts, ashstorms roll across the plains, and everywhere the air smells of smoke and tallow. Here a death is not an ending but a banking of the fire, and the people watch the pyres for who the flame might give back. The dead are said to still walk the ashstorms, and the Rekindled themselves return through resonance no one fully understands.

nodes.title: Natbakka
nodes.summary: Natbakka is a world half-hidden in mist and wholly preoccupied with fate. Mist shrines and omen towers loom out of the fog, whisper songs and omen bells sound from places no one can quite locate, and the Din read the future in everything from birdflight to the curl of smoke. It is a fatalistic world, weighed down by the conviction that what is coming is already decided, and that the most one can do is learn its shape before it arrives.

nodes.title: Neriad
nodes.summary: Neriad is a world beneath the water, lit by bioluminescent tides and built of coral. Coral palaces and abyssal domes spread across the seafloor, whale-song harmonics roll through the deep, and the Merkith move through it all in clans linked closer than blood. Above the inhabited reefs and domes lie the deepwater voids, the lightless trenches where the leviathans and the abyss horrors dwell, and where the tide-linked clans do not willingly go.

nodes.title: Oroniel
nodes.summary: Oroniel is a world held aloft on spirit and song. Spirit towers and floating sanctums drift among airy heights, resonant choirs and ethereal tones carry between them, and the Spireborn live in close communion with the spirits and elements that move through their world. It is a serene, exalted place on its surface, and a perilous one underneath, because the same openness that lets a Spireborn call a spirit also leaves them open to what the spirit might do in return.

nodes.title: Pandemonium
nodes.summary: Pandemonium is not a world so much as a cell. It is the fractured abyss beneath reality, the prison-realm raised to hold Discord, and everything about it bends toward that purpose. Impossible black cathedrals lean at angles that hurt to follow. Flesh-metal fortresses breathe. Chained void structures hang in a sky that is less a sky than an absence with weight. There is no food here, only soul vapors drifting through the dark, and the air carries a hollow buzz beneath which something vast and patient rumbles.

nodes.title: Sable
nodes.summary: Sable is a world where the story is real and the telling has teeth. Mythic amphitheaters and story-pillars rise across it, narrative opera and spoken epics fill the air, and the Bogle live and die by the tales they keep alive. A story well told here can take on a life of its own, for better and for worse, and the line between a legend and a living thing is thinner than anywhere else in the Sphere.

nodes.title: Scitnix
nodes.summary: Scitnix is a world that wants to understand everything and is afraid of what that will cost. Arcane observatories and sigil labyrinths sprawl across it, resonant chimes and coded rhythms carry messages no outsider can parse, and the Corvid spend their lives gathering, recording, and guarding knowledge. It is a world of watchers, where the act of observation is itself a kind of power, and where some things, once known, cannot be unknown.

nodes.title: Sellenia
nodes.summary: Sellenia is a world of eternal moonlight, beautiful and quietly wounded. A single great moon hangs motionless above it and never sets, silvering forests of white-leaved trees and vast mirror-still lakes. The reflections in those lakes do not always obey the sky. Stairways continue beneath the surface, and windows show rooms that no longer stand. Where moonlight crosses the right surface, memory-script surfaces across stone and bark for a few silent moments before it fades. The world feels refined and graceful, and every reflection suggests that part of Sellenia still exists in a layer just beyond ordinary sight.

nodes.title: Shadakar
nodes.summary: Shadakar is a world adrift on the Abysm, a living ocean of mist where memory, oaths, and identity are not held inside a person but worked into the water itself. Inkstone towers and warped ports cling to what solid ground remains, hanging libraries sway above the fog, and out past the harbor lights the mist closes over everything. Distant horns and ocean dirges carry across it, and no one is ever sure how far away the thing sounding them really is.

nodes.title: Shervinaw
nodes.summary: Shervinaw is a world grown from the roots up. Root-cathedrals and bark fortresses rise from a tangle of living wood, wooden flutes and root harmonics drift through the green dark, and the Shrewish live in deep communion with the roots that hold their world together. They summon from the living wood and the fungus beneath it, and they tend the root with the care of a people who know exactly what happens when it begins to rot.

nodes.title: Sporion
nodes.summary: Sporion is a living fungal world where growth and decay hold in balance, and where every death feeds something new. Towering mushroom spires rise hundreds of feet into humid, spore-filled skies, vast canopies glow in blue, green, amber, and violet, and bioluminescent growth lights the winding paths below. It is one of the oldest ecosystems in the Sphere, threaded through by an immense mycelial network that many believe carries information between every living thing in the world.

nodes.title: Sylvanoth
nodes.summary: Sylvanoth is a world where the green fights back. Thorn keeps and living hedge cities grow rather than stand, their walls a snarl of bramble and vine trained into shape by generations of patient, wary cultivation. The Arboril live inside that growth, surrounded by a wilderness that is never entirely tame and never entirely safe. Gentle reed instruments drift through the groves, an oddly soft sound for a place where the plants have teeth.

nodes.title: Thaloryn
nodes.summary: Thaloryn is a world of long winters and longer hunts. Timber halls and antler lodges stand against the cold, hunting horns and war chants roll across the wilds, and the Elken follow the great herds through a land that gives nothing for free. Survival here is the hunt, and the hunt is everything, so that a good season and a bad one are the difference between a clan thriving and a clan that quietly disappears.

nodes.title: The Scere
nodes.summary: The Scere is a world where chance refuses to lie flat. Its landscape twists into probability structures that should not hold their shape, paths that lead somewhere different each time they are walked, and skies that seem to be deciding what to be. Broken melodies drift through it with no clear source. To outsiders it feels cursed. To the people who live there, it is simply honest about something every other world hides, that nothing was ever as fixed as it pretended to be. The people who live there speak of the Scere as if it were awake, and the ones who have wounded fate the deepest stop arguing the point.

nodes.title: Therion
nodes.summary: Therion is a world where time does not run straight. Timeworn temples stand beside shifting cities that are not quite where they were yesterday, echoing drums and layered rhythms keep a beat that helps the living stay anchored, and temporal echoes drift through the streets like weather. To live here is to be careful with hours the way other peoples are careful with coin, because in Therion time is something that can be saved, squandered, and stolen.

nodes.title: Ursathar
nodes.summary: Ursathar is a world of high peaks and deep stone. Mountain halls and stone dens are carved into the cliffs and caverns, drum circles and booming chants echo through the ranges, and the Boxunin endure the cold heights with a patience as old as the rock. Strength here is not loud. It is steady, honored, and proven over a lifetime, and the worst thing a Boxunin can be is not weak by accident but dishonored by choice.

nodes.title: Valoria
nodes.summary: Valoria is a kingdom in the long process of forgetting itself. Snow lies over ruined keeps and ancient castles, blue faeliri lanterns line the roads to hold the darkness back beyond the tree line, and the halls still standing glow faintly with the residue of old resonance. It was once a single realm under one great crown, ruled by King Arandon. Now it is a scatter of fragmented territories held by councils, nobles, and desperate protectors, each clinging to a piece of a story none of them can finish telling, because the story itself has been damaged.

nodes.title: Verdantia
nodes.summary: Verdantia turns with the seasons, and the seasons are governed by courts. Giant tree palaces hold the seasonal courts, each ruling its share of the year, and the whole world cycles through them in a rhythm that is supposed to stay balanced. Seasonal orchestras play the turning, enchanted fruits ripen and fade with the months, and the Sprittle live their lives along the changing of it. Beneath the beauty runs an old anxiety, that one season might one day refuse to give way.

nodes.title: Vulkaris
nodes.summary: Vulkaris is a frozen world held in a winter that never breaks. Frozen keeps and glacier fortresses stand against the white, deep horns sound across the ice, and the wind carries frost chants from one hold to the next. The Wolfir survive here in tight clans, enduring a cold that is not cruel so much as indifferent, and their entire way of life is shaped around the single grim fact that the winter is not going to end on its own.

nodes.title: Vyrathis
nodes.summary: Vyrathis is a world that died at the top and never finished the rest. Gothic manors and blackstone towers stand over hanging crypts beneath endless crimson moons, and a fallen aristocracy still keeps court as though the centuries since their ruin were a long tedious season rather than the end. Each night the Crimson Haze rises, a blood-red mist that seeps from fractured leylines and drifts through the ravines and the streets, and after dark whole regions belong to it rather than to the living.

nodes.title: Wildermire
nodes.summary: Wildermire is a world woven on leylines. Silkwood pagodas and leyline towers rise among glowing threads of power that run beneath everything, and the whole of Foxel civilization is strung along that lattice like beads on a wire. String instruments and windchimes carry on the breeze, and the air hums faintly where the leylines run close to the surface. It is a graceful world, and a precarious one, because everything the Foxel have built depends on a network they do not fully control.

nodes.title: Wylv
nodes.summary: Wylv is a world of golden citadels and volcanic towers, an empire built on flame and the certainty of its own greatness. Ceremonial dragon horns sound across the heights, draconic beasts wheel above the lava rails, and everything is arranged to display power and rank. The Dragis rule here under a strict imperial hierarchy, and their proudest boast and deepest fear are the same thing, that a realm of fire endures only so long as it keeps burning.

nodes.title: A Court of Ashes
nodes.summary: For decades the manor on the Dreadveil cliffs belonged to Avenessa and Abbigale, a Vyra and the ghost bound to her, feared and respected in equal measure by the village of Cael'Morren below. Then one dusk the Crimson Haze came late, and a burned courier was dragged dead against the warded gate wearing the crest of a Vyrathe court wiped out two centuries before. He had one word left in him. Valis.

nodes.title: Aegros
nodes.summary: A mountain in the far north of Vyrathis, split open from within, a red mist leaking from the wound that is close to the Crimson Haze and not quite the same. The old gate lies buried under centuries of fallen stone, and a new fissure has opened in its place, pulsing with deep blood light. Beneath the mountain lie the dead courts the Crimson Haze keeps for its own, the Court of Ashes among them. Inside it is a grand and rotting hall that descends where it should climb, hung with portraits that flicker between pristine and ruined and watched by statues that weep red sand. At the end of it stands the broken throne where Kethra Valis was bound. With Kethra and Selathi passed on and Vaelen Morgrave driven out, the throne stands empty and the mountain belongs to Avenessa and Abbigale now, the Court of Ashes at its heart theirs to keep or remake.

nodes.title: Cael'Morren
nodes.summary: An overgrown village hanging off the edge of the Dreadveil cliffs on Vyrathis, poor and ill-favored, watched over by the manor above it. The manor once belonged to a Vyrathe lord until the villagers pulled him down, and Avenessa and Abbigale have held it since, respected and feared as the Vyra in the mountain manor and her pet ghost. Old Vyrnna keeps a hovel in the village below.

nodes.title: The Hanging Garden
nodes.summary: A ruined watchtower on the road north, wrapped in crimson vines, where the Crimson Haze thins for no clear reason. Bodies hang inside it on threads of memory tied to the tower's top, their spirits straining against the pull and unable to break free, every thread running back to Kethra. Higher still the threads tangle into golden strands across the sky, the melted remains of barriers that once ringed the world and shattered, dangerous to touch.

nodes.title: The Veinspire Pools
nodes.summary: A swamp of shallow crimson pools standing stagnant under a thick Crimson Haze, north past the crossing to Aegros. The water shows those who pass their own possible futures, and the Veinwraiths rise out of it wearing those futures, mocking in stolen voices and reaching to drag the living down. The Fell broke free by taking the wraiths into themselves, which woke the world's Ashletting brand in both of them and left the Crimson Haze able to burn them for the first time.

nodes.title: Heirs of Rot
nodes.summary: For decades the manor on the Dreadveil cliffs had belonged to Avenessa and Abbigale, ever since the village below pulled down the Vyrathe lord who held it and the two of them moved into the empty rooms. The people of Cael'Morren respected them and feared them in equal measure, and told their children stories about the Vyra in the mountain manor and her pet ghost. Most never came close enough to learn how near the truth they were.

nodes.title: The Broken Throne
nodes.summary: The Veinwraiths had Abbigale. They swarmed her where she stood among the pools and dragged her down toward the red water to drown her into one of them, because she had refused what they offered. She beat at them and could not move, pinned under the weight of her own possible futures, and it was Avenessa who reached in and hauled her out of the press by main strength.

nodes.title: The Weeping Root
nodes.summary: Kethra Valis sat the broken throne and named herself again as the Fell came near, the red light moving in her veins. "I am the wound. I am the weeping root. I am Kethra, bound but not broken."

nodes.title: Echoes Below Burhallow
nodes.summary: The Echochurn is the oldest sound in Burhallow, the buried beat that carries luck from one end of the Harfolk warrens to the other, and lately it has begun to skip. When it skips it reaches, and what it reaches for is the Fell. Four strangers wake in an abandoned root cellar with the dirt humming wrong around them and no memory of how they came to be there.

nodes.title: Bunny Bay
nodes.summary: A stretch of seacoast a way out from Thimblehill, past Bindler Cross, where the warren keeps a grist mill turning beside the water. The shore is cut with tide caves that fill and drain with the sea, their mouths stacked with stones piled like little shrines. Something living dens in the deepest of them. When the mill's grindstone went missing, dragged off through the woods on a trail of spilled grain, it was in one of those caves the Fell found it, in the keeping of a grub grinder trying to flood its own grotto. The miller of Bunny Bay is a grateful sort who pays honest coin and flour for a good turn.

nodes.title: The Fellwatch Tower
nodes.summary: A ruined watchtower on a hill above Thimblehill, its stone gone soft and wrong, smoke still rising off it from fires long dead. Fell held it once, an age before the strangers the Churn pulls in now, drawn into Burhallow the same way and gone the same way. What they built has been overrun, and what they were has not entirely left.

nodes.title: The Forest Glade
nodes.summary: A stretch of wood west of Thimblehill, on the road toward Bindler Cross, where the ground turns damp and the air hangs thick with drifting spore. Mushrooms grow everywhere here, glowing in the dark under the canopy, and the fungus-touched of the warren say the spores sing if you listen the way they listen. At its heart stands a ring of mushroom stalks grown tall as trees, a council of sorts, where those who can hear the fungus go to commune with it. The ring keeps its own temper. Disturb the rite and the stalks turn red, the day seems to fall to night, and something rises out of the fungus to put the trespass right.

nodes.title: The Forgotten Burrowmouth
nodes.summary: A tunnel sealed and left alone north of Thimblehill, the kind of place Harfolk parents tell their children to stay out of. It begins like any warren tunnel, soft spirals and warm glowroots and the smell of turned earth, then the glowroots start to keep a hard, even time and go dark, and the deeper it runs the more it drinks sound, until a voice comes back wrong or not at all.

nodes.title: Thimblehill
nodes.summary: A Harfolk warren built into green hills above the root cellars, all round rooted doors, houses woven like blankets, bell-shaped mushrooms, and chimneys carved from acorn and rootbone. It is the kind of place that smells of honeyroot and runs on shared meals and the steady beat of the Echochurn underneath it. When the Fell first climb to the surface, Thimblehill is setting up its spring Echobloom Festival, and the festival is going wrong. The singers fall a beat behind, the dancers lose the rhythm and catch it too late, the glowroots pulse the wrong way, and the air has turned to copper where it should be sweet. The Echobloom flowers, which open in time with the Churn, have shut themselves and will not bloom.

nodes.title: The Crooked Beat
nodes.summary: The first thing any of them knew was the beat, and it was not a song. It was a pressure low in the chest and lower in the dirt, a thump and a thump and a dry scratch between, all of it out of time with itself. Then the rest arrived. The ache of a body set down hard after being carried a long way, the smell of onion bread and wet moss, and the slow understanding that none of them had any idea where they were.

nodes.title: Where the Beat Broke
nodes.summary: The beat under the cellar had not settled. It swelled instead, louder and more crooked, until it filled the whole room, and when it broke open again three of the four strangers were simply gone. Olivia stood alone among the glowing tubers for a moment that stretched too long, and then the Churn coughed up others in their place. A shrew-small something that darted behind the barrels before anyone got a look at it. Two brothers, Birch and Mozzle, who came up arguing and poking each other and glad to be on the wrong side of the universe together. The small one turned out to be Pippa, a mouse no taller than a carrot, who climbed Olivia's leg and took up residence on her shoulder once she decided the tree was a friend.

nodes.title: The Voiceless
nodes.summary: The Churn was not finished moving people around. It pulled Kray-Thor ShadowMaw and Echo back out of wherever it had put them and set them down in the cellar again, took the others, and in the gap it left it coughed up two more. Usagi, a Harfolk hunter who, unlike any Fell before her, woke knowing exactly who she was and where she had come from, a city of her own people somewhere out in Burhallow, with no idea at all why the Churn had reached for her. And Valen, a mage who came up barefoot and reaching for a wand he did not remember owning. Mozzle was along too, more asleep than awake, the way he usually was.

nodes.title: The Corrupted One
nodes.summary: By the time the Fell reached the bottom of the Burrowmouth there were more of them than the tunnels had ever held at once. The Churn had been pulling them in and letting them go for days, and now it seemed to want all of them, the ones who had been here since the cellar and the ones it had only just dropped in. Olivia and Birch and Mozzle and Pippa. Kray-Thor, who had remembered somewhere along the way that his kind ate the dead and had stopped pretending otherwise. Echo. A blade-singer named Acantha who came up out of nowhere with a duelist's rapier and a southern warren behind her. A walking suit of enchanted iron that called itself Steelheart. A raccoon throwing hexes she made up as she went, and a few more besides.

nodes.title: The Empty Watch
nodes.summary: They came up out of the Burrowmouth into daylight and a warren that wanted to thank them. The Drumseer was home, the beat ran true again, and for once the Harfolk of Thimblehill looked at the Fell as something other than a problem the Churn had dropped on them. Even Gratcha Willowbind softened, which from her was close to a parade. The Fell seemed to be a permanent fixture now, she allowed, so she had a use in mind.

nodes.title: The Scabbard Wall
nodes.summary: Olivia grew back into the world from a root by the tower door, and Pippa was up her shoulder before she had finished arriving, telling her not to leave her alone with the rabbits again. The Churn had spun her out and spun her back, and there was no more sense to be made of it than that. Mozzle and Birch were glad to have the tree among them again, and the four of them went in.

nodes.title: The Husk Warden
nodes.summary: The Spire was working again. With the corrupted drakelith dead and the beat running true, the pillar at the tower's heart had come back to itself, lit and charged and drawing Fell into Burhallow the way it was built to instead of casting out ghosts. That was how Acantha arrived, stepping into the round hall out of nothing, a blade-singer with a dueling sword and a name Mozzle half remembered and Birch knew outright. Acantha the cult slayer. In a world this small, word of someone who hunts the hollow choirs gets around. Pippa climbed her the way she climbed Olivia and introduced herself, and the band took her in without ceremony, because that is how the Churn does things and they were used to it.

nodes.title: The Mushroom Whisperer
nodes.summary: Bubbles and Popsockle came up the hill to clear the Fellwatch and found it already clean. The beasts were gone from the yard, the dust was disturbed, and somewhere between one breath and the next the Churn had taken Steelheart and Thrain back out of the world. What was left was a quiet tower with the work already done.

nodes.title: The Missing Millstone
nodes.summary: Three Fell came awake in the room with the lit pillar, dragged back into Burhallow with the dizziness still on them. Steelheart, a knight of living iron who answered to the Unbreakable, was on his feet first and announcing he would protect the others before anyone had said there was anything to fear. Valon, a mage with a wand holstered at each hip like a gunslinger, squinted at him and pointed out that the room was quiet. Usagi, a bunny who had been through Burhallow's worse rooms before, just got up and said here we go again.

nodes.title: The Root of the Problem
nodes.summary: The Fellwatch had doors the Fell had never opened, and they spent the start of the day learning the tower's doors had opinions. One stayed shut until Acantha asked it politely, and then its handle turned itself and let them through, smiling in a way none of them could see. Another threw Mozzle off his feet when his shadow-magic reached under it, the room behind it refusing him outright. The moment it did, a page stamped itself onto the board in the next room out of nowhere.

nodes.title: The Vine Choker
nodes.summary: The thing that had taken Birch was a vine choker, a tangle of living creeper grown large and old down in the dark under the fallen tree, and it had wound him up and dragged him under before anyone could reach him. Then the Churn turned, the floor of the cavern wheeled around once, and Birch was simply gone from the world, pulled out the way the Churn pulls Fell out. In his place it set down Olivia and Popsockle, blinking and confused among the trampled thorns. Mozzle felt for a moment that he had a brother somewhere and could not place where, and let it go. Luck ran in the family. He trusted it.

nodes.title: Lore Pending
nodes.summary: Arcs whose world is not yet recorded in canon gather here until the vault names their Stratum.

nodes.title: Of Withering Thorns
nodes.summary: Three strangers woke in a dying grove with no memory of how they came, a Fribbit who named himself The Harold, a suit of enchanted armor called Frank that stood and walked on its own, and a child-shaped light named Emperion. The Needledrags came out of the undergrowth before they had names for each other, and the three fought because the alternative was worse. What they had walked into was a jungle rotting from the root, and a Council that had let it happen.

nodes.title: The Croaking Cauldron
nodes.summary: A tavern in Rib'Xotal and Gleep's favorite place in the world, a low loud room where frog musicians beat turtle shells, the regulars stay drunk on swamp water by various names, and plates of sushi ride lily-pad belts in slow circles around the floor. The food is bad and the drink is worse, and somewhere in the back, if you know to look, sits a merchant named Gib'Do who knows things worth trading for. The Fell more or less wrecked the place on their first visit and were told never to come back.

nodes.title: The Rib'Xotal Archives
nodes.summary: The vaults beneath the Council Hall of Rib'Xotal, behind great stone doors locked with the wooden spears of carved frog guardians. Inside, the swamp gives way to clean warm water and clear air, and orbs of light float above a pool, each holding a different working of the bloom-magic and the memories the Fribbit chose to lock away. One of those memories is a hundred years old, a council of elders out among the Xochitli working a rite that flared the blooms blue and then withered them black. A golden frog statue stood at the center of it all until the Fell carried it off. The archives are watched by stone guardians that wake when the vault is disturbed, and the Fell woke them twice.

nodes.title: The Rootheart
nodes.summary: A sinkhole deep in the eastern jungle, wide as a town and old as the first bloom. A slope of fallen stone runs down to a door that should not open and does, and below it lies a black pond and the Rootheart itself, a Xochitli flower grown to the size of a hall, glowing and dying at once. The Council named it the backup site of the first seal, the second place they could close the rift if the first failed, and kept it secret from everyone they ruled. The flower is failing now. Its light gutters, its base is cracked, and it leaks magic into the water. Guardian vines circle it, and the way down does not end at the flower. It opens into the rift beneath, a rotted mirror of the jungle where the thing the seal was meant to hold has a face and a voice.

nodes.title: Xul'Tec
nodes.summary: The oldest ruins in Mireth, where the first Xochitli ever bloomed, deep in unclaimed jungle past the lake and the vine-choked paths. Stepped pyramids stand green and cracked under moss, and the roots of the first blooms have grown down through the stone over the long years. Gib'Do named it as the place Vorthaz had gone, and a hermit on the road hinted the truth waiting there was something other than the Fell had been told. They reached its edge with Longsloths guarding the grove, and fought through to get inside. Within they found the truth the Council had buried, a mural carved to sell the lie of Vorthaz, a tomb meant to hold him that held nothing, and the vision that named the Council for what they did. They left it over the bodies of the cleaners sent to keep them quiet, carrying a map to the Rootheart.

nodes.title: Welcome to the Jungle
nodes.summary: Three strangers woke in a bed of glowing flowers in the heart of Mireth's jungle. The flowers were a pale blue and they pulsed like a slow heartbeat, and the light in them was going out, each beat dimmer than the one before. The Xochitli, the frogfolk called them, and they were dying.

nodes.title: Conveyor Carousel
nodes.summary: The Fell rested, took what the first fight had taught them, and set out with Gleep and Za'Mak for Rib'Xotal, the largest Fribbit city this side of the jungle. Gleep talked the whole way, promising tall trees and glowing vines and advising everyone to breathe through their mouths once the swamp rot set in. Za'Mak said less, only that the Fribbit trust nothing they do not already know, outsiders least of all.

nodes.title: The Longsloths
nodes.summary: With the golden statue in hand, the Fell went back to the Croaking Cauldron, where Gleep and Za'Mak were still making amends to the barkeep for the wreck of the night before. Gib'Do was waiting at the window. Harold tapped the glass and held the statue up, and the merchant came out through it in a tangle of limbs and tongue and landed flat in the dirt, then picked himself up and reached for his prize. The Fell held it back until he paid. Vorthaz the exile, he told them, had gone to the ruins of Xul'Tec, where the first Xochitli ever bloomed, and meant to finish what he had started there. Then he took the statue, and in trade gave them a golden augur, a coin set with a gemstone that lights a path to wherever you tell it to take you. He warned them not to get jumped on the way, wished them luck, and hopped back to his soup.

nodes.title: Revelation
nodes.summary: The jungle opened on the ruins at last. Xul'Tec stood in a clearing the light barely reached, stepped stone gone soft with moss, and torches still burning along the steps, which meant someone had been here and not long ago. The augur had gone dark when the fighting started outside, but there was no mistaking the place. This was where the first flower bloomed.

nodes.title: The Bloomwither
nodes.summary: They argued it over the bodies at Xul'Tec and chose the Rootheart. The Council could wait. Whatever the map was guarding, marked in the Council's own hand for council eyes only, was worth seeing before they kicked in any doors. Gleep said he had been there once, that it was a big hole in the ground with good bugs, and gave it three and a half out of ten. Frank looked at the map a single time, folded it away, and led them straight east through the jungle without a wrong turn.

nodes.title: The Bloom Breaks
nodes.summary: They had slept as well as anyone could in the belly of a dying god, and when they woke the Bloomwither was still there, still reaching. There was a way out to look for and a council to settle with, but the thing in front of them was the thing in front of them, and they had not come this far to leave it breathing. They went back in.

nodes.title: The Corrupted Council
nodes.summary: With the Bloomwither broken and the corruption draining back into the earth, Emperion knelt at the dying pulse of it, worked the brand, and found he could take hold of the current and turn it. He gathered the party and rode it up the way they had come, the lot of them set down at the pool where they first climbed in, Gleep complaining the whole way.

nodes.title: Shaper of the Nexus
nodes.summary: Three strangers woke in the glass wastes of Pyranthia with their memories scrubbed to nothing, a cook with no name, a penitent Vate, and a Rysen child who was hungry before she was anything else. Behind their eyes the Interface opened on its own, the hidden magic of the world, naming the dangers and keeping a quiet tally of what they did. They had barely put down their first Warped Beasts when Renka Ironstride of the Charter found them and knew what they were before they did.

nodes.title: Ashenrest
nodes.summary: A village that went silent, its people taken a few at a time by Echo Stalkers wearing the shapes of the lost. The trail of the disappearances led to the Shrine of Ember at its heart, where the Fell killed a Nexus Harbinger and found the first proof that the region was riddled with Nexus sites rather than stricken by a single one.

nodes.title: Ashveil Hollow
nodes.summary: A low place in the glass-sand desert of Pyranthia where the sand drifts gray instead of bright, and where the three Fell of this campaign woke with no memories. Warped Beasts haunt the hollow, and it was among them that the three first fought, and first met Renka Ironstride.

nodes.title: Emberstone
nodes.summary: A frontier hold built around one of the few stable points in a stretch of Pyranthia the nexus is steadily eating. It is where the three Fell were brought after waking, where they first met the Wayward Charter and the Seamwrights, and where they first saw a projection of Zarathis. The nexus tear that opened outside its walls began everything that followed.

nodes.title: The Arcane Spire
nodes.summary: A needle of old ambition rising out of the glass, the seat of the Seamwrights' sharpest minds and home to the Nexus Theorists. The Fell came to it for answers about the Fragmented Nexus Stone and got them, at the cost of the Spire itself. A Nexus Bubble formed around the stone, the scholars were corrupted, and the Fell cut their way out through transformed acolytes and the Archmage Veyrin.

nodes.title: The Astral Garden
nodes.summary: A place of impossible calm grown at the center of the Astral Realm, the dreaming space behind the nexus, guarded by Astral Guardians and Shard Golems and drifting Echo-Wraiths. At its heart waited the Astral Reflection, a piece of Zarathis's mind made solid. It was in the Garden that the Fell learned the true scale of his work, the folding of every world into one.

nodes.title: Varyndell
nodes.summary: A kingdom further out in Pyranthia, Zaeli's homeland, raised over the deepest Nexus breach in the region without knowing it. By the time the Fell reached it the borderlands had gone to corruption and the royal guard had turned. Beneath its throne room, behind a Nexus barrier, sat the Dark Console, an Anchor Node tied to Zarathis. Varyndell was where the Fell first stepped out of the world and into the Astral Realm.

nodes.title: Waking in Ashveil
nodes.summary: Three strangers woke in the glass wastes of Pyranthia with no idea who they were.

nodes.title: The Shaper's Influence
nodes.summary: The tear outside Emberstone did not stay empty. Nexus creatures came through it in a steady ugly tide, and the three held the wall alongside Renka and the Charter's people until the last of them was put down. In the wreckage of the fight they found the first of the things that would shape the rest of their lives in Pyranthia, a Fragmented Nexus Stone, a shard that hummed wrong in the hand and pulled at the Interface behind their eyes.

nodes.title: Heroes Too Soon
nodes.summary: They came back to Emberstone as heroes, and that was the start of the trouble. Fell who rose this fast were supposed to be impossible, and the leadership of both the Charter and the Seamwrights began to ask how three newcomers with no memories were outpacing veterans of decades. None of them knew what the LoreMaster had done, the boost that let the three gain Lore at twice any normal rate. The societies only saw the speed of it, and speed without a history to explain it made powerful people nervous.

nodes.title: The Arcane Spire
nodes.summary: The Arcane Spire rose out of the glass like a needle of old ambition, the seat of the Seamwrights' sharpest minds. Magister Veyrin received them, he and the circle he led, the Nexus Theorists, scholars who had spent their lives on the question of the seams. They took the Fragmented Nexus Stone and bent everything they had toward it, and what they found turned the floor out from under the three.

nodes.title: The Fall of Varyndell
nodes.summary: They went to Varyndell with Zaeli and found her homeland already lost in everything but name. The borderlands had gone over to the corruption, the land itself reshaped, and the three fought through it mile by mile to reach the capital. What waited inside the walls was worse. The royal guard had been turned, men and women Zaeli had known her whole life now standing between her and her own throne, and there was no talking any of them back.

nodes.title: The Astral Garden
nodes.summary: The Console took them out of the world entirely. They came through into the Astral Realm, the dreaming space behind the nexus, where thought had weight and the dead did not lie still. Echo-Wraiths drifted through it, the remnants of minds the seam had swallowed, and Astral Guardians and Shard Golems stood watch over the deeper reaches, set there to keep anyone from reaching what lay at the center.

nodes.title: The Shaper's End
nodes.summary: The two powers put the three on trial in everything but name. They called it a council, sat the Fell down in the war room of Varyndell, and demanded to know by what right three memoryless newcomers had broken into a sovereign kingdom, killed an Archmage, and gone where the societies themselves had not been invited. The Charter and the Seamwrights had been stepped past at every turn and they were furious about it, and the longer the questioning ran the clearer it became that the council cared more about who held the authority than about the thing pulling their world apart.

nodes.title: The Crimson Heir
nodes.summary: The Fell woke at a crossroads in Vyrathis among kneeling statues of the Forsaken, red mist leaking from the cracks in their stone necks, called through the fog by something they could not name. Edrath, last servant of the Prophet Malachar, led them to a mausoleum lit by soul-fire to meet his master, the first of the Vyrathe and a father made of ash. Malachar's own heir meant to drink the Crimson Haze down to its dregs that night and breathe the world back out unmade. The oath that forged the heir kept Malachar from raising a hand against his blood, so he needed the Fell to do it for him, and to bring up the one weapon that could.

nodes.title: The Temple of the Still Flame
nodes.summary: A temple of Valoria that Malachar captured and grew into the side of Vyrathis, hidden in a cave east of his manor. Within it lies a pool of white mist and a flame that does not move, beating slow and steady in time with the breath of whoever enters. The still flame is one of the few places a Calamiter can be unmade and rewritten. A blade bound to one purpose can be carried into the fire and held there through the pain until its curse loosens and turns toward a new name. It was here that the Fell cleansed the Pale Blade of the curse that bound it to kill Malachar, so it could be set against his heir instead. When the work was done the flame went out, and the blade came out of it changed.

nodes.title: The Crimson Heir
nodes.summary: The Fell woke at a crossroads in Vyrathis with the crimson light too thin to throw shadows, scattered across pale stones among kneeling statues of the Forsaken, red mist leaking from the cracks in their stone necks. None of them knew how they had come, only that something had called them through the fog. One of them, the bat-winged Echo, kept a single splinter of memory, a child he could not place and a father's voice somewhere out in the haze. The others woke clean. Mori was a Grimalkan who already wore a silver mark she could not account for. Lavena was a Wyspar dancer who remembered dying more clearly than she remembered living. Mayu, a Frivet of the swamp-witch sort, arrived late and took the strangeness in stride.

nodes.title: The Drums of Fellwinter
nodes.summary: Fellwinter had come to Valoria again, and two nights into the cold a sound started under King Calevar's hall. Twelve beats, always twelve, and with each twelve the torches guttered and something woke a little further. A handful of Fell woke in the frozen Veiled Swamp with no memory and no doubt they had been brought against their will. They found Whip at a cracked portal arch in the snow, turning Sunderbane over in his hands, who sent them on to the king since the drums were the king's trouble and none of his.

nodes.title: Shroomy's Phantom Delights
nodes.summary: A tavern built into a great skull, kept by the Capling Shroomy, that sits in the seam between worlds rather than on any one of them. Warm light, the clink of cups, and easy laughter spill out of it, and those who reach its door tend to feel they were invited. It can be reached from the old Smithlock arch in Valoria's Veiled Swamp, woken by the keystone, and it is where Whip came to rest after he slipped off Valoria, lodged there between worlds with Sunderbane still on his hip.

nodes.title: The Drums of Fellwinter
nodes.summary: Fellwinter had come to Valoria again, the deep cold that settles under the snow and presses the past up against the present, and a year of it had not made the kingdom love it any better. King Calevar had a reign to rebuild and a season that fought him for it, and now, two nights into the cold, a sound had started under his hall. Twelve beats, always twelve, a rhythm like drums struck underwater, and with each twelve the torches guttered and the stone shivered and something woke a little further. He wanted it silenced before Fellwinter carried it any deeper.

nodes.title: The Shattering of Valoria
nodes.summary: Four strangers woke in the Whispering Woods with no memory of how they came, pulled into Valoria by something that wanted them there. Garrik the Old found them with a lantern and a warning, and told them of Eldervale at the forest's edge and the sickness eating through it. The woods did not let them leave clean. They walked out with a pack of Rendrals howling at their backs and a kingdom coming apart ahead of them.

nodes.title: Castle Valoria
nodes.summary: The seat of King Arandon and the throne of all Valoria, shut and forgotten for centuries after Brannis erased the king and the realm lost its memory of him. A graveyard grew up before its gates in the years it stood empty. A great statue of Arandon watches over the yard, and Brannis hid Sunderbane inside it, the one blade that could undo what he had done. The Fell came here at the end of the shard hunt, where the Veiled Queen meant to reforge the Crown and take the throne. They killed her on its steps, and with Calevar restored and the realm's memory returned with him, the castle opened again for the first time in an age.

nodes.title: Eldervale
nodes.summary: A small village at the edge of the Whispering Woods, stone and timber houses gathered around an old church and a watchtower. Once peaceful, now wasting under a sickness with no source and no cure. The streets stand half empty, the windows boarded, the people grey and thin. Mayor Elara Thorn holds what remains together.

nodes.title: King Arandon's Castle
nodes.summary: The seat of Valoria's last whole reign, and the hall where Lord Brannis drove Sunderbane through King Arandon and erased him from the world. The blow that fell here shattered the Crown of Valoria, frayed the realm's memory, and broke the queen who became the Veiled Queen. It is the wound at the center of everything that has gone wrong in Valoria. The Veiled Queen has named it as the place she wants Calevar and all five shards brought to her. Whatever she means to do with the crown, she means to do it where she lost everything.

nodes.title: Sanctuary Arvonis
nodes.summary: An ancient temple in the Western Waste, raised to honor Valoria's first king and long tended by pilgrims who came to cleanse themselves at the Chalice of Purity. The Veil has it now. Shadow tendrils crawl the walls and floors, the old wards are failing, and the guardians have turned from their purpose.

nodes.title: The Altar of the First Kings
nodes.summary: An ancient stone altar in a clearing deep in the Whispering Woods, carved with the symbols of Valoria's royal line and sacred to it. Pilgrims once came here. On this altar the first shard of the Crown of Valoria was laid, and Calevar, the Rekindled King, was called back into the world.

nodes.title: The Broken Spire
nodes.summary: A ruined watchtower on a jagged cliffside, also called Brannis' Tower, abandoned after Valoria's fall and claimed by Lord Brannis as his fortress. Crumbling stone wrapped in dark moss and darker energy, braced with crude barricades, its halls a maze of broken stairs and ruined chambers. Brannis kept his laboratory at the summit and his failed experiments in cells below. With Brannis dead and the second shard taken, the Spire stands empty of its master, though the Veil's mark on the place remains. The Fell later returned to the empty tower to broker peace between Lady Seraphina and the rebellion, and it was here, on the ground where Brannis fell, that they cut the Queen's shard from Seraphina's skull.

nodes.title: The Cradle of Embers
nodes.summary: A volcanic waste in the far north of Valoria, a lake of lava ringed by ash and bare stone, and the resting place of the fifth and last shard of the Crown. A fortress cut into the rock stands in the folds of the lake with a small burning city built up around it, the Ember Fort, held by Vaelric. Everyone who lives here is a warrior. Cinder Knights in black obsidian hold the gates, rune-marked soldiers called the Volari Remnants work the streets, and hooded Cinderbound carry glowing staves among them. The city fights for its life on one front without rest, against the Pyrefused, the burning dead that haul themselves out of the lava at the lakeshore, kept back by fire-throwing engines bolted along the waterline. The Fell reached the Cradle on Maven's road and cut their way through the knights at one of its gates. The last shard, and Vaelric, lie somewhere inside the fort.

nodes.title: The Silver Veil Highlands
nodes.summary: Green, rain-soaked country on the western road out of Seraphina's lands. Kind travel, right up until the tree line gives way to the Veiled Queen's forest.

nodes.title: The Sunken Cathedral
nodes.summary: A grand ruin half drowned in the veiled swamp, its spires and arches breaking the dark water like the bones of a forgotten age. It was once the seat of the Council of Five, abandoned and overtaken by the swamp and the Veiled Queen's corruption. Its doors hang shattered and the dead lie around them, and a spectral choir sings in its flooded halls. The fourth shard of the Crown of Valoria rests somewhere within, kept by the cathedral's bound warden. The Fell reached its threshold and made camp before crossing it.

nodes.title: The Veiled Queen's Forest
nodes.summary: A dark wood where her presence sits thick enough to lean on. Paths stop behaving like paths. She baits travelers with illusions, and what looks like shelter is a snare. The snares tend to wear the shape of her own loss, grief dressed up as a scene a kind traveler cannot walk past. The forest borders her own realm, the shadow place that sits between Valoria and Pandemonium.

nodes.title: The Veiled Swamp
nodes.summary: A drowned country of standing water and dead trees, wrapped in fog and the stink of rot, where the creatures called Murmock rise out of the mud and old runes still pulse green along the paths. The line between the living and the dead is said to run thin here. The Fell crossed it to reach the Sunken Cathedral and the fourth shard of the Crown, fighting through Murmock along the way and warned by a child's ghost that the cathedral ahead was hungry.

nodes.title: The Verdant Hills
nodes.summary: Green rebel country in Lady Seraphina's lands, where Alaric Thorn's people have made their stronghold. The road in passes scouts who fear their own lady and at least one village left burning by her soldiers, the cost of a war the lady calls a rebellion and the people call survival. The rebel camp itself is wary and well watched, though it can be talked into rather than fought into by anyone with enough nerve and a bad enough disguise.

nodes.title: The Whispering Woods
nodes.summary: An ancient forest on the edge of Eldervale, known also as the Whispered Ashwood and the Rotwood, steeped in a curse older than living memory. No flame ever burned it. Erasure did, back when Discord's shadow brushed Valoria during Malvaro's last stand, and the people who died there were never allowed to finish their stories. The trees grew up through the residue of those unremembered lives, blackened and leaning wrong, and the sound that runs through the wood is the dead reaching for endings no one will let them tell. That is the whispering, and the curse, that draws the unwary deeper. The Fell woke here, summoned by something that wanted them in Valoria. The woods birth Rendrals from their own corruption, and somewhere within them stand a half-buried ruin of Valoria's first kings and the ancient altar of the royal line.

nodes.title: A Shattered Legacy
nodes.summary: They woke in the Whispering Woods with no memory of how they came to be there. Four strangers pulled out of wherever they had been by something that wanted them in Valoria, lying on the cold floor of a forest that swayed without wind. Mist hung at their ankles. The trees leaned in. Underneath it all ran a sound like voices too low to make into words, and the longer the Fell listened the more the whispering drew them deeper into the dark between the trunks.

nodes.title: The Broken Spire
nodes.summary: The road to the next shard opened inside Calevar. Back in Eldervale, weak from his waking, he clutched his head as another memory surfaced, a broken tower on a cliff and a man inside it who dealt in dark and consuming power. Brannis. The traitor still lived. He was holding a ruined watchtower called the Broken Spire, and Calevar could feel a shard of the crown there the way a man feels a splinter under the skin.

nodes.title: Sanctuary Arvonis
nodes.summary: They brought Serena home to her mother. The road from the Broken Spire ended at Lady Seraphina's halls, and the Fell came through the doors with the woman's daughter walking changed beside them, her arms half turned to a Dreadpelt's. Seraphina took one look at what Brannis had done and the room went cold. There were hard words before there were soft ones. Whip did the talking, trading and bargaining the way he does, and by the end of it Seraphina had her daughter back and a charge for the Fell. Find the Chalice of Purity. Cleanse what Brannis made of Serena, and lift the Veil from Calevar while they were at it, if such a thing could be done. Willo carried the map that would take them there. They chose to rest a night before the road.

nodes.title: The Chalice Guardian
nodes.summary: The cheated door let Willo and Lantern out into a hall with no light but the one that hung over the Chalice of Purity. It sat on a pedestal at the far end, and the air around it was wrong, too still, the kind of quiet that waits for you to make a mistake. Willo had felt the door take something as it pulled them through. She did not yet know what.

nodes.title: The Silver Talon
nodes.summary: The Fell came back to Lady Seraphina's silver halls with their purpose in pieces. Serena met them fully herself again, no trace of the beast in her, and that should have been worth something. Seraphina was not a woman moved by gratitude alone. She heard out what it had cost and what they had lost, and she took the measure of Willo in particular, the one who had paid the most and come away with the least.

nodes.title: Brokering Peace
nodes.summary: Morning came over the rebel camp with bad news. Alaric called the Fell together and told them there was a spy among his people. If word of the staged death reached Seraphina before the trap was set, the whole plan died with it. He went to finish his own preparations and left the Fell to find the traitor.

nodes.title: The Veiled Swamp
nodes.summary: With the peace at the Broken Spire holding behind them, the Fell turned to the next shard of the Crown. Willo's map showed two left, one in the veiled swamp and one in the Cradle of Embers, and the swamp was the nearer of the two. They set out at dusk and walked through the night into a country of drowned trees and standing water, fog hanging low and the air thick with rot.

nodes.title: The Sunken Cathedral
nodes.summary: The Fell came to the cathedral doors three strong. Lantern had stayed back at the camp to rest, so it was Whip, Willo, and Bullsworth who went in after the shard.

nodes.title: Alden the Faithful
nodes.summary: Lantern caught up to the others at the cathedral, rested at last after sleeping clean through the swamp and the fights in it. With him back the Fell were four again, and the bargained sentinel pushed the rune-door open and waved them into the last chamber.

nodes.title: Maven's Emporium
nodes.summary: With Alden trailing them in silence and one shard left to find, the Fell turned north toward the Cradle of Embers, the volcanic waste where the last piece of the Crown waited. The land past Seraphina's holdings gave up its trees and then its hills until there was nothing but stone and ash, a gray country under a sky the sun could not reach.

nodes.title: The Ember Fort
nodes.summary: Dawn came gray over the ash. The Fell woke in the wasteland with the Cradle of Embers close now, and Lantern woke last, the way he always does. The others had given up trying to rouse him on time and started waking early instead, and they let him have it again. Willo added his stone-deep sleeping to the growing list of things that were simply true about him. The stone travels as the stone wills.

nodes.title: The Betrayer
nodes.summary: The Fell were already inside the Cradle. They had cut their way in the night before and slept in the streets, and now they read Willo's map, the one Maven had enchanted, and watched it pull tight on a single building with a green mark burning over it. The guards along the lakeshore never looked up from the burning dead they were holding back. The Fell walked to the door, Bullsworth shoved it open, and an alarm went up.

nodes.title: Grave of the King
nodes.summary: The road ended at a graveyard. King Arandon's castle stood beyond it, Castle Valoria, shut for centuries, forgotten by a kingdom that could no longer remember the king. The graves were old and the yard was empty, the dusk sky streaked red, the air cold enough to bite. At the foot of a great statue of the king the Veiled Queen waited, and with her the Veiled Herald, Lady Serena, and Calevar, the last two held on her threads.
